channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Asiatic cheetah population shows slight increase in Iran despite ongoing conflict
item.description: Amid the turmoil and devastation caused by conflict in Iran, there is a small but significant sign of hope in the country's wildlife. This year, Iranian conservationists have recorded a slight increas...

item.title: Delhi’s draft electric vehicle policy risks favouring wealthy car owners and worsening traffic
item.description: The Delhi government's draft electric vehicle (EV) policy, issued on 11th April, aims to promote electric mobility through incentives, road-tax waivers, and restrictions on petrol and diesel vehicle r...

item.title: FedEx and KAM Collaborate to Strengthen Kenya’s Manufacturing and Export Potential
item.description: In Nairobi, FedEx in partnership with the Kenya Association of Manufacturers (KAM) hosted a high-level Industry CEOs Breakfast to discuss how efficient, time-definite logistics can catalyse Kenya's in...

item.title: Casual Social Connections Reduce Loneliness in Older Adults
item.description: Regular, low-intensity social interactions, often termed 'weak ties,' can have a meaningful impact on the mental health and well-being of older adults. Psychologists highlight that casual friendships—...

item.title: Elon Musk Sues OpenAI Over Alleged Broken Promises About Nonprofit Mission
item.description: For one federal court for California, Elon Musk and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman don face each other for courtroom over wahala wey fit affect AI industry well well. Musk claim say e con dey misled about Open...

item.title: Government proposes levy on tech giants to fund Australian journalism
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has unveiled the details of a new levy targeting major tech companies like Meta, TikTok, and Google that fail to enter commercial agreements to pay Australian news publ...

item.title: Kingston police investigate incidents of pedestrians struck by air rifle pellets from vehicle
item.description: Kingston police are currently investigating two separate incidents in which pedestrians were struck by pellets from an air rifle fired from a moving vehicle. The first incident occurred at the interse...

item.title: Schools advised to avoid outdoor activities during peak summer heat
item.description: As Chennai continues to experience a significant rise in temperatures, the State School Education Department has issued an advisory to ensure the safety of students, teachers, and staff. The circular ...

item.title: Governor Makinde Holds Meeting with Peter Obi and South-East Leaders on 2027 Elections
item.description: Oyo State Governor, Seyi Makinde, don host one-on-one meeting with Peter Obi, the presidential aspirant for African Democratic Party (ADC), alongside senior South-East political leaders. The meeting n...

item.title: Sandiganbayan Dismisses P5.3M Forfeiture Case After Respondents Settle Payment
item.description: The Sandiganbayan has officially dismissed a P5.3-million forfeiture case involving former government officials after they agreed to return the full amount to the government. The case centered on Robe...

item.title: Stanley Osifo Enters APC Presidential Race Ahead of 2027 Primaries
item.description: Stanley Osifo, wey be trader from Edo State, don officially show interest to contest for the All Progressives Congress (APC) presidential ticket ahead of the 2027 general elections. On Tuesday, he go ...

item.title: Nine Narok Entrepreneurs Awarded Sh1.9 Million in Predators' Den Programme
item.description: Nine enterprising Kenyans from Narok County have successfully bagged a total of Sh1.9 million through the Predators' Den initiative, which supports startups with training and financial aid. These winn...

item.title: Family discovers Lerutla crash docket missing after victim's death
item.description: The family of Sandra Mathabathe Mamaila, who tragically died in a car crash on May 14, 2021, were stunned to learn through media reports that the docket relating to her death had been closed and was m...

item.title: Police investigate suspected arson at London memorial wall
item.description: Counter-terrorism officers in London are currently investigating an incident in which a memorial wall was allegedly targeted in a suspected arson attack. The memorial, which honours victims and signif...

item.title: NHAI directs contractors to implement heat safety measures for workers
item.description: The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) has issued comprehensive safety guidelines to protect workers amid ongoing heatwave conditions. All contractors, concessionaires, and toll collection ag...

item.title: Madhya Pradesh Medic Asks Wife to Clean Ambulance After Patient's Blood Stains
item.description: In Katni, Madhya Pradesh, a controversial incident has come to light where a local medic reportedly asked his wife to clean an ambulance that had been stained with a patient's blood. The situation has...

item.title: Federal Health Minister and Alberta Discuss Private Practice Rules Amid Bill 11 Implementation
item.description: Federal Health Minister Marjorie Michel has raised concerns with Alberta over Bill 11, legislation allowing physicians to split their time between public and private health systems. Michel emphasized ...

item.title: Experts raise concerns over US 'zombie filler' cosmetic procedure using donor fat
item.description: A growing trend in the United States involves injecting fat from deceased donors into living patients to enhance facial and body features, a practice dubbed 'zombie filler'. While lipofilling using a ...

item.title: Two Men Arrested for Illegal Firearm in Vihiga Granted 14-Day Detention by Court
item.description: In Vihiga, two men, Evans Otieno Ongere and Abel Onyango Owuor, were arrested for possession of an illegal firearm along the Kisumu-Busia highway. They were presented before the Principal Magistrate's...

item.title: Linett Kamala Reinvents Maypole Dancing with Bass, Lights and Urban Beats
item.description: Linett Kamala, a British-Jamaican DJ, artist and educator, has revitalised the traditional maypole dance by integrating it with dancehall, drum’n’bass and contemporary visual elements. Initially encou...
